homeDescription: Welcome to my portfolio, here I showcase my best projects and technical work.
aboutDescription:
I’m Wendell, a game developer from Brazil who really enjoys programming and solving problems.

I’ve been working professionally with Unity and C#, contributing to commercial games and real production environments with international teams. Over the years, I’ve built strong experience developing scalable systems, high-performance gameplay, and production-ready UI, always focused on solving complex problems and delivering reliable results.
draftFeverSynopsis: "Draft Fever Bowl is a competitive multiplayer football management simulation game."
draftFeverDescription: Acted as the team's front-end technical reference and onboarded four programmers while architecting the scouting and drafting systems, synchronizing 2,000+ entities and raising performance from 40 to 80-100 FPS. Led the HDRP-to-URP migration, rebuilt the character-generation pipeline to cut load times from 4 minutes to roughly 50 seconds, and resolved async texture-generation frame drops along the way.
thePlatformSynopsis: "The Platform is a cross-platform user-generated content game where players create, share, and explore multiplayer experiences across PC, Mobile, and VR."
thePlatformDescription: Joined as a senior reinforcement on a cross-platform user-generated-content platform in the style of Roblox and Rec Room, brought in to diagnose and resolve critical stability issues across PC, Mobile, and VR ahead of a high-stakes Gamescom (Germany) showcase. Fixed a critical late-joiner crash in the FishNet networking layer and deployed dedicated servers via PlayFlow Cloud to support cross-platform multiplayer testing.
pixSynopsis: "PIX Football Manager is a nostalgic, casual football management simulator focused on scouting."
pixDescription: Built core gameplay systems and a scalable, data-driven UI with Unity's UI Toolkit, including diegetic interfaces that keep players immersed in the simulation. Integrated the game with backend services through a Swagger-mapped REST API, turning live data into responsive, production-ready screens.
rogueSynopsis: "Rogue Masters is a cooperative game of up to 6 players with souls-like style combat."
rogueDescription: Owned multiplayer networking for this 6-player Steam title, using Mirror over a peer-to-peer topology to synchronize animations, projectiles, game state, and Mecanim-driven network state machines for smooth, responsive online play. Integrated Steamworks features including a room browser and friend invites, tracked down and fixed a critical memory leak in Mirror, and rebuilt the bootstrap flow asynchronously, cutting startup time by roughly 60%.
uniqKillerSynopsis: "Combine different types of bodies, combat classes, skills, weapons and skins to create unique heroes, both in appearance and gameplay, for fast PVP battles in urban environments."
uniqKillerDescription: Built weapon systems and character customization on Photon Fusion's dedicated-server architecture, supporting 12-player matches with synchronized, low-latency combat. Optimized physics with non-allocating queries to keep mobile performance stable, and used PlayFab's dashboard and CI/CD pipelines to automate build generation and deployment.
petShopSynopsis: "In Pet Shop Fever, you’ll run a bustling Hotel Simulator, where every decision counts!"
petShopDescription: Designed the core UI architecture, including screen systems, menus, modals, and animations, around an additive-scene framework that kept the project modular as it scaled. Contributed to gameplay and AI systems, integrated Addressables and Asset Bundles for optimized asset loading, and helped shape the game from early prototyping through production-ready features within a highly optimized, complex codebase.
amazonSynopsis: "Ten ordinary young people, who lived in different cities, find themselves in the Amazon “by fate” and, little by little, recognize that they have a calling to work in that place. Help our heroes save the Forest!"
amazonDescription: Sole programmer on this mobile adaptation of a well-known Brazilian children's franchise, with full autonomy over technical decisions and technology choices. Aligned scope with art, design, sound, and production to keep delivery on budget and on schedule, while expanding terrain generation, UI, character abilities, enemy AI, backend integration, monetization, and analytics, holding a stable 30 FPS on mobile.
horrorSynopsis: "Where Are You is a cooperative horror game. Your goal is to investigate haunted houses and exorcise the ghost that haunts them."
horrorDescription: Personal project built to deepen my expertise in multiplayer networking architecture, with an emphasis on clean, maintainable, and scalable code for stable player connections. Implemented core multiplayer features including lobby systems and matchmaking, plus a voice-recognition system where the game's AI interprets player speech and lets it directly influence gameplay.
origensSynopsis: "Origens is a 2D platform game where the main character takes a journey in a newly created world that evolves geologically through the ages."
origensDescription: Final project for my Digital Games Technology degree, where I owned programming, art, design, and narrative end to end. Implemented core systems such as save/load, collectibles, and localization, while producing art, animations, and cutscenes with Timeline and Cinemachine. A defining project in my growth as a programmer, it's where I learned to ship a cohesive, robust experience under real constraints, balancing technical execution with artistic direction and overall game vision.
maxProSynopsis: "This app introduces the most engaging way to improve your workout routine, developed for beginner and elite athletes alike who want to achieve their fitness with fun challenges and rewards each day."
maxProDescription: Built gameplay systems for a mobile experience integrated with MAXPRO fitness equipment, translating real-world workout input into interactive mechanics. Focused on reliable input processing and responsiveness to deliver a smooth experience across skill levels.
arrowSynopsis: "You're in a cemetery and have to shoot arrows at hordes of skeletons to survive as long as possible."
arrowDescription: Technical project built to demonstrate code quality, architecture, and problem-solving during a programming evaluation. Developed over a month of daily iteration right after a bootcamp spent working alongside senior programmers, it marked a significant leap in my technical skills and understanding of production-level systems.
topDownShooterSynopsis: "A roguelike shooter where you need to clear areas with enemies and improve your equipment to get further and further."
topDownShooterDescription: Personal project exploring modular level generation, combat systems, and progression mechanics, with plans for future release.
planeSynopsis: "A delivery-driven survival experience where you pilot a plane across a flooded world, navigating dangerous skies and managing resources in a dynamic, risk-reward driven environment."
planeDescription: Building a systems-driven prototype focused on a tight core gameplay loop: responsive, adaptable flight controls, a rarity-based loot system, and resource-driven progression. Designed a loop where players collect rare items, trade them for resources, and reinvest in aircraft upgrades, creating a continuous risk-reward cycle that drives exploration and decision-making.
overthrowSynopsis: "In a dream-themed game, you have to knock down the obstacles in your way to get to the end."
overthrowDescription: Built during my bootcamp under guidance from senior developers, with a focus on core programming concepts and solid technical foundations. Applied key systems including object pooling, event-driven architecture, cross-class communication patterns, and game-flow management, laying the groundwork for scalable, maintainable code.
fruitsSynopsis: "Face the enemies and collect all the fruits to advance to the next level in this beautiful 2D platformer."
fruitsDescription: Personal project focused on clean, responsive gameplay mechanics, with an emphasis on AI behavior and core Unity systems. Used it to explore game feel and feedback, working with animation and visual effects to deliver a fluid, stable experience.
bloodlustSynopsis: "Hide in the shadows to survive the hunt, avoid being seen by hunters and quench your bloodlust."
bloodlustDescription: Built during a 48-hour game jam, with a focus on gameplay design and programming, including core mechanics and level design.
funkinFighterSynopsis: "Imagine a mix of Street Fighter and Friday Night Funkin'. Keep up with the music to beat your opponent in a street fight."
funkinFighterDescription: Creative project exploring unique gameplay mechanics, fusing rhythm and fighting systems into one cohesive, functional experience.
jumpSynopsis: "Jump the obstacles and beat the platform challenges to advance to the next stage."
jumpDescription: Remake of my first project, built to measure my technical growth in code quality, structure, and overall development approach.
contactMeDescription: If you want to know more about my work or get in touch with me, feel free to check out my networks by clicking on any of the buttons below!
